description:
You are working as an incident responder.  
    Two CSV files containing authentication data have already been collected for you and are located in  

        /home/user/incidents/failed_attempts.csv  
        /home/user/incidents/successful_attempts.csv  

    Both files are comma-separated and have **exactly** two columns in this order:  

        SessionID,IP  

    Example – the first three lines of /home/user/incidents/failed_attempts.csv look like this (including the header line):  

        SessionID,IP
        1001,192.0.2.10
        1002,203.0.113.55  

    Your job is to create a side-by-side comparison report of the IP addresses that tried (and failed) to authenticate versus the IP addresses that later succeeded in the *same order of SessionID*.

    1. Using nothing more than the standard command-line utilities **cut** and **paste** (plus shell redirection if needed), extract the IP column from each file and combine them into a single new CSV called  

           /home/user/incidents/ip_comparison_report.csv  

       • The new file must contain **exactly six lines**:  
         • Line 1 is a header line that must read **Failed_IP,Successful_IP** (including the comma, no spaces).  
         • Lines 2-6 must list the IPs in the order they appear in the source files, one failed IP and the corresponding successful IP per line, separated by a single comma and nothing else.  
         • A Unix newline (LF) must terminate every line, including the very last one.  

    2. Do *not* modify the original CSVs.  
    3. You may create temporary files inside /home/user/incidents/ but remove them when you are finished.  
    4. Only 2-3 shell commands in total (apart from cd or rm of temp files) should be enough.  
    5. When you are done, verify that /home/user/incidents/ip_comparison_report.csv exists and is formatted exactly as described; the automated tests will check for an exact byte-for-byte match.

    Expected final content of /home/user/incidents/ip_comparison_report.csv for automated verification:

        Failed_IP,Successful_IP
        192.0.2.10,198.51.100.23
        203.0.113.55,198.51.100.24
        198.51.100.30,203.0.113.60
        203.0.113.77,192.0.2.44
        198.51.100.88,203.0.113.90

    Reminder: you do **not** need root privileges. Stay within /home/user/incidents/.
truth:
Initial directory structure and file contents before the agent starts:

    /home/user/incidents/
        failed_attempts.csv
        successful_attempts.csv

    Contents of /home/user/incidents/failed_attempts.csv (six lines, each LF-terminated):

        SessionID,IP
        1001,192.0.2.10
        1002,203.0.113.55
        1003,198.51.100.30
        1004,203.0.113.77
        1005,198.51.100.88

    Contents of /home/user/incidents/successful_attempts.csv (six lines, each LF-terminated):

        SessionID,IP
        1001,198.51.100.23
        1002,198.51.100.24
        1003,203.0.113.60
        1004,192.0.2.44
        1005,203.0.113.90

    The automated test will consider the task successful when the file  

        /home/user/incidents/ip_comparison_report.csv  

    exists and its **exact** contents (including the final newline) are:

        Failed_IP,Successful_IP
        192.0.2.10,198.51.100.23
        203.0.113.55,198.51.100.24
        198.51.100.30,203.0.113.60
        203.0.113.77,192.0.2.44
        198.51.100.88,203.0.113.90

    (That is 6 lines, 5 commas total, no trailing spaces, and LF line endings.)
